scheme_name: Subsidy to Purchase E-Bike for Journalists
page_title: Subsidy to Purchase E-Bike for Journalists

details: The scheme “Subsidy to Purchase E-Bike for Journalists” by the Department of Information and Publicity, Government of Goa, aims to provide journalists based in Goa with e-bikes at a subsidised rate, facilitating easier access to essential technology for their profession. This initiative acknowledges the crucial role of journalists in educating the public and the time they spend gathering news. Two significant benefits of the scheme are providing financial assistance for purchasing e-bikes and empowering journalists with better means to disseminate information vital for a democratic system. Two significant eligibility criteria include being a resident of Goa for at least 15 years and being a full-time working journalist for at least eight years. The scheme is implemented by the Department of Information and Publicity. Every year, the Government will grant subsidies to purchase 65 e-bikes to 65 beneficiaries based on their years of working.
benefits: Subsidy up to 50% of the value on quotation or ₹ 50,000/-, whichever is less, and the remaining 50% value shall be borne by the beneficiaries.
The payment shall be effected directly to the beneficiaries upon receipt of the purchase of the e-bike document.

The subsidy shall be disbursed in a single instalment to the beneficiary, i.e., 100% on the purchase of the vehicle and production of purchase documents, i.e., the Registration Certificate book and insurance by the Beneficiary.
Disbursement of subsidy for the purchase of e-bikes will be done on a first-come-first-served basis or a maximum of two for each organisation per meeting, also considering eligibility criteria.
eligibility: The applicant should be a journalist.
The sole livelihood of the applicant should be through journalistic activities.
The applicant should be residing in Goa for at least 15 years.
The applicant should be working for a Media organisation.
The applicant should not be more than 58 years of age.
The applicant should not have availed benefit to purchase an e-bike (two-wheeler) under any other Government scheme.

offline: The interested applicant should take a printout of the

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and enclose the copies of all the mandatory documents (endorsed, if required).

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the Director, Department of Information and Publicity, Udyog Bhavan, 3rd floor, Panaji.

After approval of the application, the Beneficiary will have to provide purchased documents (Registration Certificate book and insurance) along with bank details to release the subsidy directly to the beneficiary.
The Screening Committee, constituted under the Chairmanship of the Director of Information and Publicity, will scrutinize/sanction applications and may ask for additional documents if required.
The subsidy will be disbursed in a single instalment upon purchase of the vehicle and production of the necessary documents.

documents_required: Residence Certificate issued by the Mamlatdar.
Experience Certificate from Editor/Head of Organisation.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit for journalists to qualify for the subsidy?
faqs.answer: Yes, the beneficiary must not be more than 58 years old at the time of application.

faqs.question: Can journalists who have availed similar benefits under other government schemes apply?
faqs.answer: No, applicants who have already benefited from any other government scheme for e-bikes are not eligible.

faqs.question: What proof of employment is required for the subsidy application?
faqs.answer: A letter from the Editor or Head of the Organization confirming the applicant’s employment as a journalist for at least 8 years is mandatory.

faqs.question: How much subsidy will be provided for the purchase of an e-bike?
faqs.answer: The subsidy covers 50% of the e-bike’s price or a maximum of ₹50,000, whichever is lower.

faqs.question: Are part-time journalists eligible to apply for the subsidy?
faqs.answer: No, only full-time journalists whose sole livelihood depends on journalistic activities qualify for the subsidy.

faqs.question: How many e-bikes will be subsidized under this scheme annually?
faqs.answer: The government will grant subsidies for 65 e-bikes each year, allocated on a first-come, first-served basis.

faqs.question: Can journalists working for electronic media organizations apply for the subsidy?
faqs.answer: Yes, journalists working for both print and electronic media organizations are eligible if they meet the other criteria.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on the number of beneficiaries per media organization?
faqs.answer: Yes, a maximum of two beneficiaries per media organization will be considered per meeting.

faqs.question: Who constitutes the committee responsible for scrutinizing and approving applications?
faqs.answer: The committee includes the Director of Information and Publicity, Presidents of journalist associations, and a representative from the Finance Department.

faqs.question: Can the subsidy be used to purchase any type of e-bike?
faqs.answer: The type and cost of the e-bike will be decided by the government, and applicants must submit a quotation from a reputed dealer.

faqs.question: What is the validity period of the scheme before it is reviewed?
faqs.answer: The scheme will be reviewed after 3 years from its commencement date.

faqs.question: Are retired journalists eligible to apply for the subsidy?
faqs.answer: No, only working journalists who meet the age and employment criteria are eligible.

faqs.question: What is the role of the Mamlatdar in the application process?
faqs.answer: The Mamlatdar issues the 15-year residence certificate, which is a mandatory document for proving domicile in Goa.

faqs.question: Can journalists who engage in other professions alongside journalism apply?
faqs.answer: No, applicants must not engage in any other activity and must rely solely on journalism for their livelihood.

faqs.question: Is there a relaxation clause for any of the eligibility conditions?
faqs.answer: The government may relax certain conditions on a case-by-case basis, except those related to financial benefits.

faqs.question: How is the subsidy amount calculated if the e-bike’s price exceeds ₹1,00,000?
faqs.answer: The subsidy will be 50% of the price or ₹50,000, whichever is lower, regardless of the e-bike’s total cost.
